migrations.description: Rename all project agents to proj-* prefix to avoid built-in Explore/general-purpose capture, and fix MCP tool propagation by removing tools: whitelists from read-only agents + injecting mcp__<server>__* entries into write agents.
Switch /migrate-bootstrap skill to use migrations/index.json for discovery; fix bootstrap_repo placeholder in .claude/bootstrap-state.json.

Complete the agent rename from migration 001 by catching prose-form references missed by its narrow sed, inject AGENT_DISPATCH_POLICY_BLOCK into every dispatching skill, and normalize **Dispatch X** bold prose to the canonical subagent_type="proj-X" form.
Strengthen proj-plan-writer discipline to forbid implementation code in task files, and update /execute-plan to instruct specialists to read domain rules before writing code.

Convert skill allowed-tools frontmatter from non-standard comma-separated form to spec-compliant space-separated form. Comma form breaks with Bash(git add *) patterns. Agent tools field is unchanged (correctly comma-separated per spec).

Flatten companion nested-repo bug — detect and remove $COMPANION_ROOT/$PROJECT_NAME/.git, re-stage as files in the umbrella, and rewrite .claude/hooks/sync-companion.sh to use path-scoped git -C instead of cd into the subdir.

Fix orchestrator skill dispatch — reclassify main-thread vs forkable, enforce frontmatter contract (no context:fork on orchestrators), inject pre-flight gate, audit agent tool whitelists per role table, promote /test-fork as permanent diagnostic.
Detect and relocate orphan technique files from techniques/ at project root into .claude/references/techniques/. Fixes damage from migrations 001/005/007 which wrote to the wrong path.

Forbid built-in Explore/general-purpose/plugin agent fallback — patch CLAUDE.md + .claude/rules/skill-routing.md + .claude/rules/general.md to add always-loaded rule; strengthen AGENT_DISPATCH_POLICY_BLOCK in all dispatching skills.

Regenerate proj-plan-writer agent + write-plan + execute-plan skills with dispatch-unit batching — tier classification (5 signals), FFD packing algorithm over dep-isolated layer bins, batch file format.

Generalized force-read pattern + MCP routing rule + /audit-agents skill. Retrofit STEP 0 force-read blocks into all sub-agents, add .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md, add orchestrator skill that dispatches proj-consistency-checker to audit force-read + MCP propagation.

Enforce plan-writer packing via 3 layers: (A) agent Self-Audit process step + Tier Classification Table output + worked example; (B) /write-plan Post-Dispatch Audit gate w/ loopback cap 2 + hard-fail; (C) remove legacy task-NN-*.md format, add /execute-plan Pre-Flight Audit w/ hard-reject. Fixes 2026-04-11 regression where 5 packable micro tasks emitted as 5 separate batch files.

Fix MCP glob propagation bug — Claude Code silently ignores mcp__<server>__* glob patterns in agent tools: frontmatter. Strip tools: line from all write agents (proj-debugger, proj-tdd-runner, proj-code-writer-*, proj-test-writer-*) so they inherit MCP access from parent. proj-plan-writer explicitly skipped (intentionally MCP-free per scope lock). Updates .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md Agent layer guidance and fixes modules/06 + modules/08 audit checks that previously required mcp__*__* globs.

Complete MCP access rollout — drop tools: from proj-plan-writer so it inherits MCP like every other agent. Supersedes migration 013's defensive skip (the 'MCP-free by design' rationale was post-hoc; scope-lock restricts plan CONTENT, not tool access). Rewrites CLAUDE.md + .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md + modules/02 + modules/05 to state the absolute rule: ALL agents (read-only, write, planning) OMIT tools: to inherit parent MCP access. The theoretical hard-restriction escape hatch is kept as a footnote but marked 'currently unused by any agent in this project'.

Max Quality Doctrine — 10-layer defense against output truncation and effort inflation. Creates .claude/rules/max-quality.md rule, .claude/hooks/check-quality.sh SubagentStop hook (high-precision literal elision + effort-pad scan, self-block prevention, TODO/weeks-days delegated to Layer 6 reviewer), .claude/hooks/prompt-nudge.sh UserPromptSubmit script (SKILL CHECK + conditional MAX QUALITY on write/impl verbs), extends .claude/hooks/stop-verify.sh nudge text (no scan — Stop schema lacks last_assistant_message), updates .claude/settings.json hook wiring, syncs techniques/prompt-engineering.md + techniques/token-efficiency.md + techniques/agent-design.md to .claude/references/techniques/ (agent-design gains Hook Input Schema Gotcha + Use/Mention Discipline + Template Placeholder Conventions sections), adds @import .claude/rules/max-quality.md to CLAUDE.md, retrofits STEP 0 reinforcement + max-quality force-read across all proj-*.md agents, adds Completeness Check section to proj-code-reviewer*.md, adds calibrated-effort FORBIDDEN/REQUIRED rules to proj-plan-writer*.md. Dogfooded — plan itself complies with the doctrine it implements.

Reviewer confidence gate — suppress hallucinated external-API findings. Patches proj-code-reviewer + proj-code-reviewer-<lang> agents to require BLOCKER/MAJOR findings about external APIs/libraries/frameworks to cite a verified source (docs URL, source file path, or explicit 'VERIFIED-UNCERTAIN' marker) before report inclusion; low-confidence API claims downgrade to MINOR w/ '(unverified)' tag. Adds Confidence Gate section to agent body + force-read list. Closes 2026-04-11 regression where reviewer invented non-existent SDK method signatures as BLOCKERs.

Ship /deep-think skill — 'brainstorm on steroids' 7-phase main-thread orchestrator for multi-pass adversarial ideation. Phases: evidence scan → parallel persona-rotated divergent ideation → separate-evaluator shortlisting → deepening of top candidates → adversarial gap hunt → gap-resolution loop → dual-artifact synthesis (proposals.md + brainstorm-format spec.md). Hard caps: 3 Phase-1 passes / 5 critic rounds / 15 gap-resolution dispatches. Convergence signal: 0 HIGH-severity gaps from critic for 1 consecutive round. Self-contained migration inlines full SKILL.md + references/personas.md + references/dispatch-templates.md as heredocs, adds routing-trigger bullet to client-project brainstorm/SKILL.md Decision Tree (idempotent grep-guarded).

MCP tool routing retrofit — fixes body-prose layer that survived migration 013. Migration 013 fixed frontmatter-level MCP inheritance (drop tools: from write agents) but agent bodies still prescribed Read/Grep-first discovery as MANDATORY in Section 2 Pre-Work, outranking the soft 'prefer serena' hint in Section 6. STEP 0 force-read cited mcp-routing.md (propagation config) not mcp-tool-routing.md (action→tool table which did not exist). This migration creates .claude/rules/mcp-tool-routing.md (conditional on .mcp.json containing serena or codebase-memory-mcp), adds @import to CLAUDE.md, glob-patches every proj-*.md STEP 0 force-read list + override clause to reference the new rule, rewrites Section 2 Pre-Work in code-writer/test-writer/code-reviewer agents to MCP-first precedence, rewrites Section 6 Plugin/LSP/MCP to hard rule with action→tool table pointer + Grep/Read/Glob fallback. Body-prose patches run unconditionally (agents become MCP-aware regardless of project MCP state); rule-file creation + CLAUDE.md @import are gated on MCP detection. Supersedes external setup prompt at \\truenas-pioneer\media\software\claude-mcp-setup-prompt.md (deprecated by this migration — pointer replaces it).

Rewrite .claude/hooks/detect-env.sh with bulletproof numeric reads — fixes SessionStart:startup hook error caused by grep -c stdout concatenation and unvalidated bash arithmetic on .learnings/ counter files.

Companion repo sync deletion semantics fix — companion never propagated file deletions (additive cp -r fallback on MINGW64 where rsync is absent), causing stale files to accumulate forever and cold-start restore to resurrect deleted files. Patches 4 client-project files idempotently: (1) sync-config.sh gets _POSITIONAL/_FLAGS pre-parse, new mirror_delete() function using find+sort+comm with 50% safety gate and --force override, new export --prune flag wiring per-SYNC_DIR mirror_delete, new reset subcommand with 5s cancel window, revised status case with three-category classification (NEW/DIFF/STALE) filtering Common subdirectories diff line, USAGE line update; (2) sync-companion.sh rsync branch gains --exclude='settings.local.json', cp-r fallback replaced with nuke-and-repave (rm -rf ${COMPANION:?}/.claude/ + cp -r) for .claude/ and .learnings/ with post-copy rm -f of settings.local.json, CLAUDE.local.md copy line removed; (3) detect-env.sh wholesale companion restore replaced with explicit per-SYNC_DIR additive loop using [[ ! -d ]]/[[ ! -f ]] guards, never restores CLAUDE.local.md or settings.local.json; (4) .claude/skills/sync/SKILL.md argument-hint patched via awk (sed forbidden — literal | collides with delimiters) to add export [--prune], status description rewritten to three-category wording. Pure bash/sed/awk (zero Python3), MINGW64-safe (sed -i.bak + rm .bak), every patch grep-q guarded for idempotency, FIX2_SKIP escape hatch for sync-companion.sh anchor drift. Bootstrap source-of-truth edits in modules/06-skills.md Dispatch 07 (argument-hint + status description), modules/09-companion.md (Step 2 cross-reference + export/import spec + commands table), modules/03-hooks.md (detect-env template + sync-companion template) prevent next bootstrap refresh from reverting the fix (module regeneration ratchet).

TaskCreate observability gate for /execute-plan and /deep-think — idempotent awk patches inject a ToolSearch-loaded TaskCreate/TaskUpdate call-site into both SKILL.md files: Step 0 + Post-Execution closeout in execute-plan; post-Iteration-State gate section + per-phase TaskUpdate markers (Phases 1-6) + Phase 7 closeout in deep-think. Sentinel-guarded (literal 'ToolSearch("select:TaskCreate,TaskUpdate")'), re-run safe, no regex with '.*' anywhere (per .learnings/log.md:10-11 rule — sed anchors must be literal). Backed by the shared TASKCREATE_GATE_BLOCK definition newly added to modules/06-skills.md after PRE_FLIGHT_GATE_BLOCK; client-project bootstrap refreshes regenerate both skills with the gate in place, this migration brings already-bootstrapped projects forward without a full refresh. Fails fast on anchor drift with a clear manual-patch message — never silently half-patches.

Replace LLM-rendered skill/agent bootstrap with template-fetch from tracked templates/ directory. Modules 05+06 become thin fetch loops. Per-file SHA classification: missing files INSTALL via byte-download from upstream (no local state to preserve); existing files that differ from upstream MERGE via LLM hand-merge (preserves project-specific customizations while incorporating upstream improvements); identical files SKIP. Dry-run preview + explicit confirmation before writes; --force skips prompt only, never downgrades MERGE to byte-overwrite. No TSV sidecar — each step re-iterates manifest through jq (MINGW64 CR-safe, no trailing-\r hazard). Verify is loose for merged files (checks existence + non-empty + YAML frontmatter fence, not SHA match) since merged files differ from upstream by design.

Patch .claude/skills/migrate-bootstrap/SKILL.md to resolve the bootstrap source repo from BOOTSTRAP_REPO env var → .claude/bootstrap-state.json bootstrap_repo field → canonical tomasfil/claude-bootstrap default, instead of hardcoding the canonical slug. Enables fork users to point /migrate-bootstrap at their own migration track. Literal-string idempotency guard (no regex). Additive only — touches one file and leaves a .bak-023 backup.

Add risk classification + Failure Modes checklist to proj-plan-writer — idempotent awk patch adds ## Risk Classification section, #### Risk: line in task template, #### Failure Modes sub-section with 5 questions (production failure, detection, rollback, invalidating dependency, least-certain assumption). Sentinel-guarded on '#### Risk: {low|medium|high|critical}'. Backed by templates/agents/proj-plan-writer.md update in bootstrap repo.

TaskCompleted gate — writes .claude/hooks/gate-task-complete.sh (TaskCompleted standard event, top-level payload fields task_subject/task_description/task_id) and merges TaskCompleted entry into .claude/settings.json via Python3. Blocks marking medium+ risk tasks completed without verification evidence (verified:/tests: pass/build: pass markers). Idempotent sentinel guard on 'gate-task-complete.sh' in settings.json. Smoke-tested with 4 fixture payloads. Bypass: TASKCOMPLETED_GATE_BYPASS=1.

Reclassify proj-debugger and proj-code-reviewer from opus to sonnet per agent-design.md ANALYZES → sonnet classification principle. Both agents are read-only analyzers (proj-debugger traces execution paths and produces diagnosis text; proj-code-reviewer does structural pattern matching, RCCF compliance, cross-reference checking). Preserves effort:high (generation thoroughness via reasoning_effort, NOT thinking blocks per GitHub #31536 empirical test) and proj-code-reviewer's memory:project. Quality floor: Anthropic routing guidance routes code reviews + debugging to Sonnet tier; 4.6-generation SWE-bench gap = 1.2pt. Migration fetches updated proj-debugger.md, proj-code-reviewer.md, and techniques/agent-design.md (to .claude/references/techniques/) from bootstrap repo via gh api with sentinel-guarded idempotency. Includes SGAP-1 Post-Apply note for bootstrap self-update workflow.

Reclassify proj-verifier and proj-consistency-checker from effort:high to effort:medium per Anthropic agentic-coding guidance for procedural tool-use workloads (Build→Tests→Cross-refs→Frontmatter checklist). The frontmatter override is load-bearing post-session-default-change (effort:high was a v2.1.94 session-default artifact). Also patches templates/skills/verify/SKILL.md to add `git diff --stat HEAD` pre-dispatch scope signal (SMALL/LARGE classification forwarded to both agents) — gap 2.5-HIGH-2 fix lets the now-medium-effort agents calibrate report depth. Migration fetches updated proj-verifier.md, proj-consistency-checker.md, and verify/SKILL.md from bootstrap repo. CLAUDE.md edit is bootstrap-repo-only (client CLAUDE.md is user-maintained, not templated). SGAP-1 Post-Apply note included.

Skill class taxonomy + skill frontmatter corrections. Adds Skill Class → Model Binding section to techniques/agent-design.md (7-row table mapping main-thread inline / orchestrator / forkable bounded / forkable analysis classes to model+effort defaults). Corrects write-prompt/SKILL.md drift (opus+medium → sonnet+high; opus+medium was a commit 60c53a0 LLM inference artifact, not author-specified). Lowers audit-file/SKILL.md effort:medium → low (shell body is mechanical pre-flight only; proj-code-reviewer at sonnet+high does the actual review work). Adds Phase 0 scope constraint to deep-think/SKILL.md dispatch prompt (bounded local scan, no web search, no synthesis — corrected text from gap-res-2-effort-semantics.md §RET-HIGH-1, preserves effort:high generation thoroughness framing). Migration fetches techniques/agent-design.md to client `.claude/references/techniques/`, plus the 3 skill SKILL.md files. Step 5 (code-standards-markdown.md sync) is PENDING FOLLOW-UP — source template not located in modules/ or templates/rules/; documented as gap in .learnings/log.md and in the migration body. SGAP-1 Post-Apply note included.

Backfill effort:high justification invariant on all agent templates + install audit-agents A7 presence check. Every agent with `effort: high` must have a `# high: <TOKEN>` comment line immediately after (and every `effort: medium` a `# medium:` comment). Token vocabulary: GENERATES_CODE, SUBTLE_ERROR_RISK, STATEFUL_MEMORY, MULTI_STEP_SYNTHESIS, INHERITED_DEFAULT, DISPATCHES_AGENTS. Migration fetches 7 primary agent templates (proj-code-writer-markdown, proj-code-writer-bash, proj-tdd-runner, proj-reflector, proj-researcher, proj-plan-writer, proj-quick-check) + glob-loop awk backfill for code-writer-*.md and test-writer-*.md client variants + defensive re-fetch of proj-debugger/proj-code-reviewer/proj-verifier/proj-consistency-checker with cmp -s SKIP when 026/027 already applied + templates/skills/audit-agents/SKILL.md (A7 presence check appended). Prerequisite: migration 026 must be applied first (proj-debugger and proj-code-reviewer `# high:` comments land in 026). SGAP-1 Post-Apply note included.

Install /audit-model-usage skill + .claude/rules/model-selection.md machine-readable policy. Closes the model-effort-selection deep-think loop — migrations 026-029 updated agent/skill templates to the new policy, but drift accumulates over time as new specialists are spawned via /evolve-agents, /reflect rewrites agent bodies, or hand edits bypass the template→migration workflow. The audit skill (sonnet+medium, read-only) walks .claude/agents/*.md + .claude/skills/**/SKILL.md, parses frontmatter via Grep, joins against model-selection.md expected bindings, and emits COMPLIANT/DRIFT/UNKNOWN per row with file:line evidence. The policy rule is the single source of truth for expected (model, effort) per agent/skill class. Additive only — installs one new skill directory and one new rule file; no existing agents, skills, modules, hooks, or settings modified. Migration fetches both artifacts from bootstrap repo via gh api with sentinel-guarded idempotency. SGAP-1 Post-Apply note included.

Consolidate .claude/rules/mcp-tool-routing.md into .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md. Migration 018 split MCP routing into two rule files (propagation + action→tool table) as a minimal surgical patch on top of the legacy mcp-routing.md that only covered propagation. Practice showed the split was artifactual — one file can own both concerns without friction. Manual consolidation in the field revealed the split was fragile: agent force-read blocks referenced a file a project had already consolidated, leaving 'if present' override prose semantically dead across all 13 agents. Migration 031 is the retrofit: (a) append-only merge of Lead-With Order / Action → Tool / Gotchas / Decision Shortcuts sections into mcp-routing.md, preserving any project-specific empirical content already added post-bootstrap; (b) delete standalone mcp-tool-routing.md; (c) strip the dead @import from CLAUDE.md; (d) patch every .claude/agents/proj-*.md + sub-specialists (glob) STEP 0 force-read block, override prose, and Pre-Work step via exact-string python substitutions (NO-OP for hand-edited agents, reported for manual review); (e) fetch refreshed techniques/agent-design.md. All steps sentinel-guarded + idempotent. Prerequisite: migration 018 applied. Preserves the pre-merged case: HAS_MERGED=yes branch skips content merge but still patches agent prose + strips dead @import.

MCP freshness discipline — append 5 sections to .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md (CMM Freshness pre-flight, Grep Ban on indexed projects, Permission-Seeking Ban, Project Slug Convention, Transparent Fallback) and remove the now-redundant 'cmm project key = full path' bullet from Gotchas (promoted to its own section). Motivated by two field-observed sessions exposing recurring MCP anti-patterns: Grep-first discovery on a named type instead of cmm.search_graph + cmm.get_code_snippet, and permission-seeking on a stale cmm index instead of silent reindex. cmm server does not auto-reindex on MCP startup — it loads the last on-disk graph — so client-side pre-flight (list_projects + detect_changes + optional index_repository) is the only freshness guarantee. Rules enforce: named-symbol lookups on indexed projects MUST route through cmm.search_graph first; stale-index detection MUST trigger auto-reindex without asking permission (per max-quality.md §6); project resolution uses the path-slug convention (full absolute path with / and \ replaced by -) via list_projects, not bare folder names; legitimate MCP→text-search degradation MUST be disclosed transparently via 'MCP→fallback: {tried} → {why} → {path}' one-liner so the user can calibrate confidence. Additive only — append-only content merge with sentinel-guarded idempotency (## CMM Freshness header as sentinel), no existing sections rewritten. bootstrap-state.json advances to last_migration=032.

MCP discovery gate — project-level PreToolUse hook (mcp-discovery-gate.sh) + First-Tool Contract clause in every proj-* agent STEP 0 override paragraph + settings.json PreToolUse wiring. Motivated by three field-observed sessions violating the mcp-routing.md Grep Ban even with the rule active (migration 032): two main-thread Grep calls on C# type names in two separate sessions, and a code-writer subagent running multiple Search calls on namespace/class declarations despite STEP 0 force-read of mcp-routing.md. Root cause: rules are declarative; correct behavior depends on agents/main reading and obeying. Three post-rule violations prove rule-only enforcement is insufficient. Migration 033 installs mechanical enforcement (layer 3, the hook) and tightens the declarative contract (layer 2, First-Tool Contract clause in STEP 0). Hook: reads PreToolUse JSON on stdin, base64-round-trips the pattern to survive shell word-splitting, classifies via python3 regex (CamelCase identifier, class/namespace/interface/struct declaration, qualified type reference, I-prefixed interface, alternation with PascalCase token), gates only when .mcp.json lists codebase-memory-mcp or serena, exempts literal phrases / error messages / file-extension globs / URL-paths / quoted text with spaces / snake_case variables / markdown headings, emits JSON {decision:block,reason:...} on stdout + stderr reason + exit 2. Fail-open on parse errors (broken hook never breaks unrelated tool use). Additive only — creates hook, appends PreToolUse entry to settings.json, exact-string prose replacement in agent STEP 0 blocks (NO-OP for hand-edited variants, reported for manual review). Complements migration 032 (which added rule sections CMM Freshness / Grep Ban / Permission-Seeking Ban / Project Slug Convention / Transparent Fallback to .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md). Defense-in-depth stack: layer 1 rule (032) defines the violation, layer 2 contract (033) warns the agent in STEP 0, layer 3 hook (033) blocks mechanically at PreToolUse.

Deferred MCP discovery inventory — patches detect-env.sh (SessionStart hook) to emit a 'Deferred MCPs: ...' line enumerating every MCP server reachable from any scope Claude Code reads (project .mcp.json, user ~/.claude.json top-level, local ~/.claude.json projects.<abs-path>.mcpServers, managed managed-settings.json + managed-mcp.json + managed-settings.d/*.json per-OS, plugin ~/.claude/plugins/*/.mcp.json). Known server metadata lookup table renders each entry as '{name} ({N} tools) {purpose}' (serena 13 tools semantic code/LSP symbols, codebase-memory-mcp 15 tools graph-indexed code search, context7 4 tools library docs); unknown servers fall back to '{name} (? tools) purpose unknown — check ToolSearch'. No servers reachable → 'Deferred MCPs: none' (explicit dormant signal). Walker crash → 'Deferred MCPs: inventory check failed (<reason>)' (fail-open). Pairs with a new '## Deferred MCP Discovery' section appended to .claude/rules/mcp-routing.md that requires 'ToolSearch select:mcp__{server}__{tool}' as the mandatory first step before invoking any deferred MCP tool, forbids fabricating 'it was in the deferred list' to cover a skipped ToolSearch, and defers to the existing Transparent Fallback rule on MCP→text-search degradation. Motivated by a field-observed session where the main thread attempted a deferred MCP tool invocation, found it uncallable (schema not loaded), fell back to Grep without calling ToolSearch, and then fabricated deferred-list membership to cover the routing miss. Defense-in-depth layer 4 on top of migrations 031 (mcp-routing consolidation) + 032 (CMM Freshness / Grep Ban / Permission-Seeking Ban / Project Slug Convention / Transparent Fallback rule sections) + 033 (PreToolUse mechanical gate + First-Tool Contract): layers 1-3 tell Claude WHAT to route through for a known-available MCP; layer 4 tells Claude WHICH MCPs are deferred-but-reachable this session. Additive only — sentinel-guarded idempotency on both the hook stanza ('# Deferred MCP inventory') and the rule section ('## Deferred MCP Discovery'); existing content never rewritten. MINGW64-safe walker (absolute-path file reads, no stdin collision). bootstrap-state.json advances to last_migration=034.

Tool call hygiene — append `## Tool Call Hygiene` section to `.claude/rules/token-efficiency.md` covering two call-site token-waste rules observed in field session retrospectives: (1) `cmm.search_graph(query=…)` BM25 mode requires `limit ≤10` — unbounded `query=` returns 100-500+ hits × 10-15k tokens each (single session observed 405 + 431 hits consuming ~25-30k tokens in two calls); `name_pattern=` exact-match lookups are exempt (structurally bounded, small result sets). (2) Large log / output / fixture files require `wc -l` pre-sizing followed by scoped reads via `Read offset=… limit=…` or shell `tail` / `head` / `sed -n` — NEVER full-file Read on unsized logs; failed oversized Read still echoes error payload into context. Existing `token-efficiency.md` sections cover content compression (instructions, specs, memory files), not call-site discipline; this migration closes the gap with a new section alongside them. Single-file additive append via `split('## Output Carve-Out', 1)` insertion pattern (same as migration 032/034 Step 3), sentinel-guarded idempotency on `^## Tool Call Hygiene` header and `035` in `applied[]`. bootstrap-state.json advances to last_migration=035.

Main thread orchestrator doctrine — install advisory-only enforcement stack that reminds the main thread to delegate to sub-agents per the classify → dispatch → synthesize pattern. Creates `.claude/rules/main-thread-orchestrator.md` with Tier 0–3 definitions (Tier 0 conversational / Tier 1 Read on known exact paths / Tier 2 dispatch on investigation via `proj-quick-check` default + `proj-researcher` escalation on incomplete or multi-source / Tier 3 dispatch on code change) plus a Quick-Fix Carve-Out with five-criterion gate (single file, ≤10 lines, known target, mechanically obvious, zero cross-file impact — all must hold) and an Investigation Escalation Ladder that allows multiple sequential `proj-quick-check` calls before escalation with no hard dispatch-count limit. Creates `.claude/hooks/orchestrator-nudge.sh` — PreToolUse advisory hook on `Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit|Grep|Glob` that emits a short stderr reminder matched to the tool class and exits 0 unconditionally (NEVER blocks; fail-open on parse errors; bootstrap-repo-exempt via presence check for `claude-bootstrap.md` + `modules/` + `migrations/` at project root). Merges a new PreToolUse matcher entry into `.claude/settings.json` via python `json.load` (JSONC guard aborts with manual-patch message if `//` comments present). Appends `@import .claude/rules/main-thread-orchestrator.md` to the project's `CLAUDE.md` `@import` block via python last-import insertion (manual-review fallback if no `@import` block present). Motivated by field observation that main thread routinely bypasses `proj-quick-check` and `proj-researcher` for investigation and edits files directly that should route through `proj-code-writer-{lang}`, bloating main-thread context on Opus and triggering compaction earlier than necessary. Existing `skill-routing.md` and `general.md` rules tell main WHEN to dispatch but do not define tiers or WHEN a direct edit is acceptable — this migration installs the missing doctrine layer. Advisory-only rather than blocking per explicit design rejection of hard-block approach. Sentinel-guarded idempotency on all five steps (rule-file header, hook first-line comment, `orchestrator-nudge.sh` substring in settings.json, `@import` line in CLAUDE.md, `036` in `applied[]`). bootstrap-state.json advances to last_migration=036.

Rewrite .claude/hooks/observe.sh in-place so Bash tool events capture the command in a cmd field instead of emitting empty-field JSONL — restores semantic content for the proj-reflector / consolidate clustering pipeline. Pre-fix client projects (bootstrapped before the in-place fix) had a single codepath that wrote every tool event (Edit, Write, AND Bash) as {"tool":"Bash","file":""}; Bash tool_input has no file_path (it has command), so every Bash observation carried zero semantic payload and the reflector correctly reported "telemetry-only, no semantic content for clustering" and skipped the Common Commands cluster. Edit/Write telemetry was correct in the old version (both carry file_path). No previous migration touched observe.sh, so /migrate-bootstrap never regenerated it — pre-fix projects kept the broken hook until now. Migration is sentinel-guarded (grep for printf format '"cmd":"%s"') so it is idempotent and skips already-fixed projects. Full 48-line hook body inlined via quoted heredoc (<<'OBSERVE_SH' so $VAR, $(...), ${...} stay literal), .bak-037 backup written on first rewrite only. Smoke test feeds a Bash fixture ({"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git status"}}) and an Edit fixture ({"tool_name":"Edit","tool_input":{"file_path":"foo.md"}}) through the rewritten hook and asserts the last two JSONL lines contain "cmd":"git status" and "file":"foo.md" respectively, then truncates the smoke-test rows via pre-count head cleanup. Verify block re-runs the same fixtures plus shell-standards checks (shebang, set -euo pipefail, tool_input.command read, both printf format strings present). Historical observations.jsonl entries are NOT touched — old broken records age out via the existing 10MB rotation already present in observe.sh. Additive only (one hook file rewritten); no settings.json, agents, rules, or modules modified. bootstrap-state.json advances to last_migration=037.

CMM proactive bootstrap + zero-drift policy — installs .claude/hooks/cmm-index-startup.sh (SessionStart startup-matcher hook: reads .claude/cmm-baseline.md, compares git SHA + node/edge counts, triggers unconditional full reindex on any mismatch, fail-open). Ships /cmm-baseline skill (init / refresh / check / verify-sentinels) + references/framework-catalog.md (generic framework blind-spot catalog, no third-party MCP product names).
